mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (quarterly) signals robust aggregate demand that drives earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If real GDP growth decelerates for two consecutive quarters and S&P 500 still produces positive 20-day returns, this signal is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate rises above 4.5%, it indicates that monetary policy is becoming restrictive, which dampens economic activity and corporate profits, producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, the S&P 500 generates positive returns in more than 60% of 20-day windows, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals a contraction in consumer spending and residential investment, indicating a slowdown that will depress corporate earnings and equity prices.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS decline month-over-month
failure_condition: If both series decline but S&P 500 rises in 60% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) exceeds 110 index value, capacity constraints emerge, leading to input cost pressures that erode profit margins and outweigh output benefits, pushing equity returns negative.
failure_condition: If in periods when INDPRO > 110, the 20-day SPX return is positive in > 50% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signal ongoing labor market strength that lifts consumer confidence and spending, translating into positive short-term equity returns within a week.
failure_condition: If over a 1-year rolling window, the Rank IC of PAYEMS change vs 5-day forward returns is below 0.05, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls to 4.5% or below, the labor market is at full employment, and further strength may trigger wage inflation that forces the Fed to tighten, hurting equities in the following month.
failure_condition: If in episodes of UNRATE <= 4.5%, the forward 20-day S&P 500 return is positive more than 55% of the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) affirm a virtuous cycle of rising income and spending, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and PAYEMS increase month-over-month
failure_condition: If the joint increase coincides with negative SPX 20-day return in > 60% of occurrences, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment (CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 3%), any further increase in inflation signals overheating, prompting tighter monetary policy that depresses equity valuations over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in high inflation regimes, SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of cases, reject.
